heading: Separation at age 62: brigadier generals and rear admirals (lower half)
label: 14510 Separation at age 62: brigadier generals and rear admirals (lower half)
text: Unless retired, transferred to the Retired Reserve, or discharged at an earlier date, each reserve officer of the Army, Air Force, or Marine Corps in the grade of brigadier general who has not been recommended for promotion to the grade of major general, and each reserve rear admiral (lower half) of the Navy who has not been recommended for promotion to the grade of rear admiral, except an officer covered by , shall be separated in accordance with on the last day of the month in which the officer becomes 62 years of age.section 14512 of this titlesection 14515 of this title
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in sections 3843(a) and 8843 of this title, prior to repeal by , (c)(3).Pub. L. 103–337, § 1629(a)(3)
Amendments2006— substituted “age 62: brigadier generals” for “age 60: reserve brigadier generals” in section catchline and “62” for “60” in text.Pub. L. 109–364
Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Oct. 1, 1996section 1691(b)(1) of Pub. L. 103–337section 10001 of this title
amendments: Amendments2006— substituted “age 62: brigadier generals” for “age 60: reserve brigadier generals” in section catchline and “62” for “60” in text.Pub. L. 109–364
